channel.title: Oldies TAL Feed
channel.description: Random episode 10+ years old, updated weekly

item.title: [Oldies] 93: Valentine’s Day ’98
item.description:
https://www.thisamericanlife.org/93/valentines-day-98

Stories about couples that all take place decades after that moment their eyes first meet.

Prologue
Richard Klein of Cornell University explains that the way we view love really began with love poems in the 13th century — an illusion. (7 minutes) by Ira Glass

Act One
Richard Bausch reads his story Letter to the Lady of the House . (25 minutes) by Richard Bausch

Act Two
Linda Howard and Richard Bloom, in Boca Raton Florida, had been married years when she started flirting with another man. (7 minutes) by Stephanie Howard

Act Three
Poet Donald Hall reads about his wife Jane Kenyon, who contracted leukemia, went through treatment, and died. His book is also called Without: Poems . (18 minutes) by Donald Hall

Originally Aired: 1998-02-13
